name: task
summary: Queueable unit of work with a stable ID and typed payload, executed by its handler.
description: Creates a task class with a unique runtime ID
problem: You have work that doesn't need to happen inside the request — sending an email, calling an external API, running a slow report — and you want a typed, named unit you can queue, retry, and monitor without coupling the producer to the worker.
appliesWhen: The work can run outside the producing request
Failure should be retryable independent of the producing context
Payload is a fixed shape that travels through a queue
You want runtime introspection (queue depth, retry counts) per task type
avoidWhen: The work must complete before responding to the user — run it inline
It's a one-time CLI ops task — use phpnomad/command
The reaction is fire-and-forget with no payload — use phpnomad/event + phpnomad/listener
examples: Add a task to send a welcome email asynchronously
Queue a task that syncs an order to the warehouse system
Create a background job for generating monthly reports
tradeoffs: Adds a queueing layer compared to inline execution. Worth it when the work is slow or unreliable; overkill when it isn't.
outputs: A {name} task class implementing the Task interface
Stub typed payload properties to fill in
postApply: Add typed properties for the task payload
Pair with phpnomad/task-handler to define the worker logic
Wire the dispatch from the producer side once both halves exist

name.aiHint: Verb-noun PascalCase. Describe what the task does (SendWelcomeEmail, SyncOrderToWarehouse).

taskId.aiHint: Lowercase, dot-separated, namespace-prefixed. Must be unique across the project.
